quiz.title: SAT English: Unit 5 Quiz 3
quiz.subtitle: Goal-Oriented Writing — Selecting the Option That Best Accomplishes a Specific Task (10 Questions)
quiz.subject: SAT Preparation

questions.question:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
  • In 1928, Alexander Fleming discovered penicillin by accident in his laboratory.
  • He noticed that a mold called *Penicillium notatum* had contaminated a petri dish and killed the surrounding bacteria.
  • This discovery led to the development of the world's first true antibiotic.
  • Before antibiotics, common infections like pneumonia were often fatal.
  • Fleming's work earned him the Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine in 1945.

The student wants to emphasize the significance of Fleming's discovery. Which choice most effectively uses information from the given notes to accomplish this goal?
options: Alexander Fleming's 1928 discovery of penicillin earned him a Nobel Prize in 1945.
By leading to the world's first antibiotic, Fleming's discovery transformed medicine and saved lives from once-fatal infections.
While working in his lab, Fleming noticed that a common mold had killed bacteria in a contaminated petri dish.
Fleming discovered penicillin in 1928, a time when pneumonia was still a common and dangerous illness.
questions.explanation: Choice B is correct because it explicitly connects the discovery to its global 'significance'—transforming medicine and saving lives. Choice A focuses on the award, which is a recognition of significance but not the significance itself. Choice C describes the event (the 'how') rather than its importance. Choice D provides a historical context without fully articulating the impact of the discovery.

questions.question:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
  • The 'Great Green Wall' is an African-led initiative to combat desertification in the Sahel region.
  • The project involves planting a 5,000-mile-long belt of trees and vegetation across the width of Africa.
  • Beyond environmental restoration, the project aims to create jobs for millions of people.
  • It also seeks to improve food security by restoring 100 million hectares of currently degraded land.
  • Launched in 2007, it is one of the most ambitious climate actions in the world.

The student wants to highlight the socioeconomic benefits of the Great Green Wall. Which choice most effectively uses information from the given notes to accomplish this goal?
options: The Great Green Wall is a 5,000-mile-long environmental project designed to stop desertification in Africa.
Launched in 2007, the initiative seeks to restore 100 million hectares of land to improve the climate.
By providing millions of jobs and improving food security, the Great Green Wall offers vital support to local communities.
The project involves planting trees across the Sahel to restore land and combat the effects of a changing environment.
questions.explanation: Choice C is correct because it specifically addresses 'socioeconomic benefits' by mentioning job creation and food security. Choices A, B, and D focus primarily on the environmental or physical aspects of the project (planting trees, stopping desertification, land restoration).

questions.question:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
  • The 'Enigma' was a cipher machine used by the German military during World War II.
  • Alan Turing and his team at Bletchley Park worked to crack the Enigma code.
  • They developed 'The Bombe,' an electromechanical device that could rapidly test different settings.
  • Breaking the code allowed the Allies to anticipate enemy movements and shortened the war by years.
  • Turing is now considered the father of theoretical computer science and artificial intelligence.

The student wants to emphasize the impact of Alan Turing's work during World War II. Which choice most effectively uses information from the given notes to accomplish this goal?
options: Alan Turing, the father of artificial intelligence, led a team at Bletchley Park to build 'The Bombe.'
By cracking the Enigma code, Turing's team provided intelligence that shortened World War II and saved lives.
Turing developed an electromechanical device used to decrypt messages sent by the German military.
The work at Bletchley Park involved testing different settings on the Enigma machine to reveal secret codes.
questions.explanation: Choice B is correct because it focuses on the 'impact' of his wartime work—shortening the war. Choice A highlights his long-term legacy in computer science. Choice C and D focus on the technical process of his work rather than the broader impact requested by the goal.

questions.question:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
  • The 'Antikythera Mechanism' is an ancient Greek device discovered in a shipwreck in 1901.
  • It consists of a complex system of over 30 bronze gears.
  • X-ray imaging has revealed that it was used to track astronomical positions and eclipses.
  • Dating back to the 2nd century BCE, it is often called the 'world's first analog computer.'
  • No other technology of similar complexity appeared again for over a thousand years.

The student wants to emphasize the unprecedented technological complexity of the Antikythera Mechanism. Which choice most effectively uses information from the given notes to accomplish this goal?
options: Discovered in a 1901 shipwreck, the Antikythera Mechanism was an ancient Greek device used to track the moon.
The device used over 30 bronze gears to track eclipses, representing a level of complexity not seen again for a millennium.
Known as the world's first analog computer, the Antikythera Mechanism dates back to the 2nd century BCE.
Using X-ray imaging, scientists discovered that the bronze gears of the Antikythera Mechanism were quite complex.
questions.explanation: Choice B is correct because it combines the specific evidence of complexity (30 bronze gears) with the fact that it was 'unprecedented' (not seen again for a millennium). Choice A and C provide historical context but do not emphasize the technological gap. Choice D mentions complexity but fails to highlight just how unique or 'unprecedented' the technology was compared to later eras.

questions.question:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
  • The 'Great Blue Hole' is a giant marine sinkhole off the coast of Belize.
  • It is circular in shape, over 300 meters across and 124 meters deep.
  • It formed during several episodes of quaternary glaciation when sea levels were much lower.
  • The site was made famous by Jacques Cousteau, who declared it one of the top scuba diving sites in the world.
  • It is part of the Belize Barrier Reef Reserve System, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

The student wants to explain the geological origin of the Great Blue Hole. Which choice most effectively uses information from the given notes to accomplish this goal?
options: The Great Blue Hole, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, was declared a top diving destination by Jacques Cousteau.
Measuring 300 meters across, the Great Blue Hole is a massive sinkhole located within the Belize Barrier Reef.
The Great Blue Hole formed during periods of glaciation when lower sea levels created a massive inland sinkhole.
Jacques Cousteau helped make the Great Blue Hole famous after exploring its 124-meter-deep waters.
questions.explanation: Choice C is correct because it directly addresses the 'geological origin' (formed during glaciation due to lower sea levels). Choice A and D focus on the site's fame and exploration history. Choice B provides physical dimensions but does not explain *how* or *why* it formed.

questions.question:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
  • The 'Timbuktu Manuscripts' are a collection of hundreds of thousands of African documents.
  • They cover a wide range of subjects, including astronomy, medicine, law, and philosophy.
  • Written primarily in Arabic and local languages like Songhay and Tamasheq, they date back to the 13th century.
  • These texts prove that Timbuktu was a major intellectual hub long before European colonization.
  • Local families and librarians hid the manuscripts for centuries to protect them from invaders and climate damage.

The student wants to highlight the intellectual diversity of the Timbuktu Manuscripts. Which choice most effectively uses information from the given notes to accomplish this goal?
options: The Timbuktu Manuscripts, dating to the 13th century, were written in languages like Arabic and Songhay.
To protect the documents from damage and invaders, local families hid the Timbuktu Manuscripts for centuries.
The Timbuktu Manuscripts demonstrate that the city was an intellectual hub well before the era of European colonization.
Ranging from astronomy to law and philosophy, the Timbuktu Manuscripts cover a vast array of scholarly subjects.
questions.explanation: Choice D is correct because it lists the various fields of study (astronomy, law, philosophy) to specifically demonstrate 'intellectual diversity.' Choice A focuses on language and age. Choice B focuses on the history of their preservation. Choice C highlights the city's status as a whole but is less specific about the diversity of the *manuscripts' content* than Choice D.

questions.question:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
  • The 'Seed Vault' on the island of Spitsbergen, Norway, is designed to withstand natural and man-made disasters.
  • It is located 130 meters above sea level to remain dry even if the ice caps melt.
  • The facility is carved into solid permafrost, which maintains a constant temperature of -18° Celsius.
  • Its primary mission is to provide a 'safety net' for the world's biodiversity in case of catastrophic crop failure.
  • The vault currently houses over one million distinct seed samples from nearly every country on Earth.

The student wants to emphasize the vault's resilience to climate change. Which choice most effectively uses information from the given notes to accomplish this goal?
options: Located in Norway, the 'Seed Vault' houses over one million samples to prevent global crop failure.
The vault is positioned 130 meters above sea level and carved into permafrost to ensure its contents remain safe and frozen despite rising temperatures.
The facility maintains a constant temperature of -18° Celsius and acts as a safety net for biodiversity around the world.
By housing seeds from nearly every country, the vault ensures that the world can recover from a man-made or natural disaster.
questions.explanation: Choice B is correct because it identifies specific geographical and structural features (elevation above sea level and permafrost location) that directly address 'resilience to climate change' (rising temperatures/melting ice). Choice A and D focus on the mission and quantity of seeds. Choice C mentions the temperature but omits the environmental context that explains why the vault is resilient to external climate shifts.

questions.question:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
  • Mary Golda Ross was the first known Native American female engineer.
  • A member of the Cherokee Nation, she was hired by Lockheed Aircraft Corporation in 1942.
  • She was a founding member of Skunk Works, a top-secret research and development program.
  • Her work focused on the design of interplanetary space travel and the orbiting of satellites.
  • Much of her research remains classified to this day due to its sensitive nature.

The student wants to highlight Ross's early contributions to aerospace development. Which choice most effectively uses information from the given notes to accomplish this goal?
options: A member of the Cherokee Nation, Mary Golda Ross was the first Native American female engineer in the United States.
Ross's research into satellite orbits and interplanetary travel began during her time as a founding member of the Skunk Works program.
Because much of her work remains classified, Mary Golda Ross is only recently being recognized for her role at Lockheed.
In 1942, Lockheed Aircraft Corporation hired Mary Golda Ross, who eventually became an influential engineer for the Cherokee Nation.
questions.explanation: Choice B is correct because it specifically identifies her 'contributions to aerospace development' (satellite orbits and interplanetary travel). Choice A focuses on her identity and 'first' status rather than her specific technical contributions. Choice C focuses on the classified nature of her work. Choice D focuses on her hiring date and heritage without detailing her aerospace work.

questions.question:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
  • The 'Great Hedge of India' was a massive salt-tax barrier built by the British in the 19th century.
  • It was a literal hedge of thorny bushes and trees that stretched over 2,300 miles.
  • The barrier was intended to prevent the smuggling of salt into British-controlled territories.
  • Customs officials used the hedge to ensure that the lucrative salt tax was paid by all traders.
  • Environmental historians now study the hedge as an example of large-scale colonial ecological engineering.

The student wants to explain the economic purpose of the Great Hedge. Which choice most effectively uses information from the given notes to accomplish this goal?
options: The Great Hedge of India was a 2,300-mile-long barrier of thorny bushes that is now studied by environmental historians.
Stretching across India, the hedge was an example of ecological engineering designed by British colonial officials.
The British constructed the 2,300-mile hedge to stop salt smuggling and enforce the collection of salt taxes.
Traders in 19th-century India were forced to pay taxes because of a massive hedge that prevented them from traveling freely.
questions.explanation: Choice C is correct because it identifies the 'economic purpose' (stopping smuggling and enforcing tax collection). Choice A and B focus on the physical description and historical study of the hedge. Choice D mentions taxes but inaccurately suggests the hedge's purpose was to stop 'free travel' rather than specifically salt smuggling.

questions.question:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:
  • The 'Lichtenberg figure' is a branching, tree-like electrical discharge pattern.
  • It occurs on the surface or inside of insulating materials during high-voltage breakdowns.
  • These patterns are named after the German physicist Georg Christoph Lichtenberg, who discovered them in 1777.
  • In nature, they can appear on the skin of people struck by lightning, usually fading within a few days.
  • Modern artists use particle accelerators to create these 'frozen lightning' patterns inside blocks of acrylic.

The student wants to provide a definition and the physical cause of a Lichtenberg figure. Which choice most effectively uses information from the given notes to accomplish this goal?
options: Named after a 18th-century physicist, Lichtenberg figures are tree-like patterns that modern artists create using particle accelerators.
Lichtenberg figures are branching electrical discharge patterns that form when high-voltage breakdowns occur in insulating materials.
Often called 'frozen lightning,' these patterns can appear naturally on the skin of lightning strike victims or inside acrylic blocks.
Lichtenberg figures, discovered in 1777, are unique patterns that occur on the surface of materials during high-voltage events.
questions.explanation: Choice B is correct because it fulfills both parts of the goal: it provides the definition (branching electrical discharge patterns) and the physical cause (high-voltage breakdowns in insulating materials). Choice A focuses on the namesake and artistic use. Choice C uses a nickname and focuses on where they appear rather than what causes them. Choice D mentions the date of discovery and the cause generally but is less precise than Choice B regarding the material involved.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent work! You can accurately select the option that best accomplishes a specific rhetorical goal using provided source material.
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review missed questions and focus on whether the choice directly addresses the stated goal or includes irrelevant information.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Remember: rhetorical synthesis questions test your ability to match content to purpose. Eliminate options that miss the goal.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! Ask yourself: what is the specific task, and which option uses the notes most effectively to complete it?
